title: Trump Administration Fires Immigration Judges Who Blocked Deportations of Pro-Palestinian Students
detail: The Trump administration fired immigration judges who had blocked deportation orders targeting pro-Palestinian student activists, escalating what critics described as a judicial purge of the immigration court system. The firings targeted judges at the Executive Office for Immigration Review (EOIR) who had issued rulings halting removal proceedings against students — many of them visa holders — who had participated in campus demonstrations supporting Palestinian rights. The dismissals follow a broader pattern of the administration removing or reassigning judges perceived as obstructing enforcement priorities. Immigration court judges are DOJ employees, not Article III judges with lifetime tenure, making them vulnerable to executive removal. The American Immigration Lawyers Association condemned the firings as a direct assault on judicial independence within the immigration system. Legal advocates warned that removing judges who issue unfavorable rulings sets a precedent for executive interference in quasi-judicial proceedings, chilling independent decision-making across all immigration courts. The purge accelerates a long-running effort by the administration to restructure immigration courts to align with aggressive enforcement policy, including elimination of pending cases through prosecutorial discretion and reassignment of judges to border dockets.

media.caption: Trump administration fires immigration judges who blocked deportation orders targeting pro-Palestinian students
